concepts.title: Definition of Controlling
concepts.description: Controlling is the process of ensuring that the organizational goals and objectives are achieved by evaluating, comparing, and reporting deviations in the actual performance from the standard performance and taking corrective action.

concepts.title: Steps of Controlling
concepts.description: The steps involved in controlling are: (1) Establishing standards, (2) Setting standards, (3) Measuring performance, (4) Comparing performance and (5) Taking corrective action.

concepts.title: Types of Control
concepts.description: There are three types of control: (1) Line control, (2) Staff control and (3) Functional control.

concepts.title: Difference between Controlling and Motivating
concepts.description: Students often confuse controlling with motivating. Controlling is concerned with achieving organizational goals, whereas motivating is concerned with encouraging employees to work towards the goals.

concepts.title: Benefits of Controlling
concepts.description: The benefits of controlling are: (1) Achieving organizational goals, (2) Improvement in quality, (3) Reduction in costs, (4) Increase in productivity and (5) Better decision making.

concepts.title: Process of Controlling
concepts.description: To remember the process of controlling: 'Establish Standards, Set Standards, Measure Performance, Compare Performance, Take Action' - ESMPCTA.

concepts.title: Types of Standard
concepts.description: There are two types of standard: (1) Physical standard and (2) Quantitative standard.

concepts.title: Methods of Controlling
concepts.description: The methods of controlling are: (1) Personal control, (2) Statistical control and (3) Budgetary control.
